description: Complete DesignShowcase section redesign - replace the code editor/code snippets aesthetic with a premium interactive visual experience showing website layouts assembling themselves, UI components snapping into place, and wireframe-to-design transitions. This should become the strongest visual section of the site.
steps: Completely rewrite components/Home/DesignShowcase.tsx - remove all code snippet content and the dark code editor visual
Create an interactive 'design assembly' visualization using pure CSS/SVG animations and GSAP ScrollTrigger:
- Design a browser window mockup (white/light, with a toolbar) that shows a website layout assembling piece by piece as user scrolls
- Elements should animate in from different directions: header slides down, hero content fades up, cards stagger in from below, footer rises
- Use CSS shapes (divs with border-radius, gradients, shadows) to represent UI components - NOT real images
- The assembly should be scroll-driven (scrub: true) so the user controls the animation by scrolling
Add a second visual: a grid of floating UI component cards (buttons, input fields, cards, typography samples) that have a subtle 3D float/parallax effect
Add section heading with word-reveal animation: 'From Concept to Reality' or similar
Add a short descriptor text below heading explaining the design process
The overall section should feel like watching a website come together in real-time
Use the accent color (#050FA3) sparingly for highlights in the assembled UI mockup
Ensure scroll-triggered animations use ScrollTrigger with proper start/end markers
Add a subtle background treatment (very faint grid or dot pattern at 2-3% opacity)
Ensure reduced-motion support: show the assembled state immediately without scroll animation
acceptance_criteria: No code snippets, code editor, or 'hacker aesthetic' remain in this section
The section shows a visual of UI/website elements assembling themselves
Animation is scroll-driven (user controls pace by scrolling)
All visuals are CSS/SVG-generated - no external image dependencies
Section has proper heading and descriptor text
The section feels premium and polished, not like placeholder wireframes
findings: Build and lint pass cleanly. Used ScrollTrigger with scrub:1 for the browser assembly animation with start/end at 60%/40% viewport. Floating cards use both parallax (scroll-driven y offset) and continuous bob/rotation yoyo animations. The dot grid background uses a radial-gradient at 2.5% opacity. All UI elements are pure CSS divs with borders, rounded corners, and background colors. The accent color is used sparingly on buttons and icon containers within the mockup.
